title: 5.5 The Immune System
description: Quiz covering the immune system: types of immunity, white blood cells, antibodies, vaccination, and immune disorders.

questions.question: Immunity is defined as the body's ability to prevent the invasion of _____.
The immune system includes defences against viruses, bacteria, fungal infections, and _____.
What do Pattern recognition receptors (PRRs) help to maintain?
The innate immune system consists of _____ lines of defences.
What is the role of cytokines in the immune response?
options: To attract immune cells to infection locations
questions.correctAnswer: To attract immune cells to infection locations

questions.question: The complement cascade is activated to help recognize _____.
When is adaptive (acquired) immunity formed?
options: After an initial reaction to a new pathogen
questions.correctAnswer: After an initial reaction to a new pathogen

questions.question: Vaccination is based on which immune process?
The adaptive immune system recognizes particular _____ antigens.
Immunological memory involves signature antibodies or T cell receptors that _____.
What cells can be summoned to eliminate an invader swiftly during a recurring infection?
Where are lymphocytes formed?
Where do some lymphocytes mature into T cells?
Which are the three most common types of lymphocytes?
options: Red blood cells, platelets, plasma cells
T cells, B cells, natural killer cells
questions.correctAnswer: T cells, B cells, natural killer cells

questions.question: What is the most common form of white blood cell?
A phagocyte is a cell that _____.
options: engulfs and digests cellular debris and pathogens
questions.correctAnswer: engulfs and digests cellular debris and pathogens

questions.question: Which chemical signal causes neutrophils to be recruited to the site of injury or infection?
Macrophages play a role in _____.
options: both innate and adaptive immune systems
questions.correctAnswer: both innate and adaptive immune systems

questions.question: Which of the following is a target of macrophages?
Macrophages stimulate which cells to respond to pathogens?
Dendritic cells serve as _____ between the innate and adaptive immune systems.
Dendritic cells are frequently found in _____.
options: tissues that contact the outside world
questions.correctAnswer: tissues that contact the outside world

questions.question: Where do dendritic cells travel in response to infections?
Antibodies are also called _____.
Antibodies are _____ shaped proteins.
Which cells produce antibodies?
Antibodies bind to antigens on the surface of foreign substances and recognize them as _____.
Lymphokines are the common name for _____ produced by the immune system.
What effect do steroids and corticosteroids have on the immune system?
A mother breastfeeding her baby provides antibodies to the child. This is an example of _____.
